iteration_notes: Verified Mike Collins' 2026 FEC Senate candidate ID S6GA00390 and principal committee C00544684 on FEC.gov and retained those records as correctly belonging to Mike Collins.
Corrected Mike Collins' education entry from University of Georgia to Georgia State University based on his official congressional biography.

Removed Mike Collins top_donors entries that were tied to the wrong OpenSecrets CID and left the array empty because reviewed accessible sources did not provide directly retrievable, source-verifiable named top contributors for his Senate committee within the tool constraints.
Verified Jon Ossoff's vote on PN1522 directly on Senate.gov and retained it as a 'no' vote, adding a more specific official source.
Verified Jon Ossoff's H.R. 6938 entry against Congress.gov and retained the bill title and 2026-01-15 Senate vote date while upgrading the source to a specific Congress.gov bill page.
Verified Jon Ossoff's H.J.Res. 20 vote directly on Senate.gov and retained it as a 'yes' vote with a specific Senate roll-call source and clearer description.
Removed the unverified Jon Ossoff PN12-41 Abigail Slater nomination entry that reviewers flagged as inconsistent.
Replaced Jon Ossoff Immigration sources with a specific official Senate press release documenting his detention-abuse investigation.
Replaced Jon Ossoff Foreign Policy sources with a specific official Senate press release documenting his questioning of intelligence officials about Iran.
Replaced Jon Ossoff Election Reform sources with a specific official Senate press release on bipartisan voting-rights legislation while retaining a news source for broader campaign framing.
Corrected Buddy Carter's vote on H.R. 2 to 'yes' per official House records.
Added a clarifying note to Buddy Carter's House career description that he announced a 2026 Senate run instead of seeking reelection to the House seat.
Added a clarifying note to Buddy Carter top_donors source titles that the listed figures reflect career OpenSecrets House fundraising data rather than only his 2026 Senate campaign.
Adjusted Derek Dooley's assistant-coach career entry to avoid overlap with his head-coaching years.

Verified Derek Dooley's principal campaign committee as DOOLEY FOR GEORGIA (C00914531) on FEC.gov but kept top_donors empty because accessible fetched FEC pages did not expose itemized contributor names and amounts in retrievable text.
Strengthened Derek Dooley's low-confidence issue summaries on reproductive rights, election reform, and foreign policy by explicitly noting that no detailed platform was found in reviewed materials.
Reframed Mike Collins' Foreign Policy stance to clarify that cited war-powers votes were cast in the 118th Congress and may not fully reflect his 2026 Senate campaign positions.
Reframed Mike Collins' Tech & AI stance to lead with the absence of a defined AI-regulation platform and treat the deepfake ad only as campaign context.

Searched for additional Q1 2026 public Georgia Senate polling; no clearly retrievable additional independent poll was confirmed, so the race description now notes that public polling remained limited as of late March 2026.
title: 2026 U.S. Senate election in Georgia
description: Georgia will elect a U.S. senator in 2026, with Democratic incumbent Jon Ossoff seeking a second term. The race is expected to be highly competitive because Georgia remains a closely divided battleground state and Republicans are trying to unify behind a nominee after a contested primary. Key contrasts in the race center on Ossoff’s incumbency and fundraising against Republican candidates who are competing to consolidate conservative voters and appeal to swing voters in the general election. The primary is scheduled for May 19, 2026, with a runoff on June 16 if needed; the general election is on November 3, 2026, with a runoff on December 1, 2026 if no candidate wins a majority.

candidates.name: Jon Ossoff
candidates.summary: Jon Ossoff is the Democratic incumbent U.S. senator from Georgia, first elected in 2020 and seeking reelection in 2026. Before joining the Senate, he worked in investigative media and as a congressional staff aide, and he has centered his Senate work on issues including health care costs, housing, infrastructure, and oversight of federal agencies.

Healthcare.stance: Ossoff supports lowering health-care costs, including prescription drug prices and access to affordable coverage. He has backed Medicare drug-price negotiation, bipartisan measures to speed generic-drug approvals, and extensions of Affordable Care Act tax credits.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff Helps Pass Bipartisan Bill into Law to Lower Drug Costs for Georgians
Sen. Ossoff Urges Republicans to Put Politics Aside, Extend Affordable Care Act Tax Credits

Economy.stance: Ossoff emphasizes domestic manufacturing, supply-chain investment, and job training. He has highlighted federal investment in Georgia industries and supported measures aimed at lowering housing costs and expanding workforce development.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff Working Across the Aisle to Bolster Georgia’s Economic Development
Sen. Ossoff Helps Pass Bipartisan Legislation to Lower Housing Costs
Sen. Ossoff Introduces Bill to Help Georgians Unlock Job Training Opportunities Across Georgia

Climate/Energy.stance: Ossoff supports clean-energy investment and energy-security measures. He has promoted federal support for Georgia solar manufacturing and bipartisan legislation to modernize energy infrastructure and lower energy costs.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff Announces Historic Federal Investment to Strengthen Georgia Solar Manufacturing
Sens. Ossoff, Dr. Cassidy Introduce Bipartisan Bill to Strengthen American Energy Security

Reproductive Rights.stance: Ossoff supports abortion access, contraception access, and IVF protections. He has publicly pushed to protect women’s access to contraception in Georgia and opposed efforts he says would restrict reproductive health care.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff Pushing to Protect Women's Access to Contraception in Georgia
Sen. Ossoff Pushes Back Against Trump Administration's Termination of IVF Programs at the CDC

Immigration.stance: Ossoff has focused on oversight of immigration detention and allegations of abuse. He has released reports on credible reports of human-rights abuses in detention facilities and called for DHS and ICE to address those problems.

sources.title: NEW: Sen. Ossoff Investigation Uncovers Over 1,000 Credible Reports of Human Rights Abuses in Immigration Detention
Sens. Ossoff, Durbin Launch Investigation into Abuse Allegations at Alligator Alcatraz

Guns & Safety.stance: Ossoff supports gun-violence prevention measures and has backed bipartisan background-check-related legislation aimed at protecting children and improving safety in caregiving settings.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff Working Across the Aisle to Strengthen Background Checks, Protect Georgia Children

Foreign Policy.stance: Ossoff emphasizes congressional oversight of foreign-policy and intelligence matters. He has questioned claims about an imminent Iranian nuclear threat and pressed intelligence officials on the distinction between intelligence findings and political assertions.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff Presses Director of National Intelligence on Imminent Threat Posed by Iran, Fulton County Raid

Social Justice.stance: Ossoff has centered much of his social-justice work on civil-rights oversight and anti-trafficking efforts. He has investigated abuses in immigration detention and supported bipartisan legislation to combat child trafficking and exploitation.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff Investigation Uncovers Over 1,000 Credible Reports of Human Rights Abuses in Immigration Detention
NEWS: Sen. Ossoff's Bipartisan Bill to Protect Children from Human Traffickers Passes U.S. Senate

Education.stance: Ossoff has promoted workforce and technical-education investment, including legislation to encourage private investment in training facilities and equipment at schools and colleges. He frames education policy around preparing Georgians for in-demand jobs.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff Introduces Bill to Help Georgians Unlock Job Training Opportunities Across Georgia

Tech & AI.stance: Ossoff has called for guardrails on government use of artificial intelligence. He has raised concerns about the Defense Department’s planned use of AI software he says has been associated with antisemitic content and other harmful outputs.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff, Colleagues Launch Inquiry into Department of Defense’s Use of AI Software Known for Antisemitic Content

Election Reform.stance: Ossoff supports voting-rights protections and opposes stricter voter-verification measures such as the SAVE Act. He has backed broader access and anti-discrimination measures in election administration.

sources.title: Sen. Ossoff Advances Key Bipartisan Voting Rights Legislation
Senators Ossoff and Warnock won’t vote for SAVE Act, Georgia context

Local Issues.stance: Ossoff has highlighted Georgia-specific infrastructure and water projects, including funding for port upgrades and planning tied to the Chattahoochee River Act. He presents these efforts as part of his work to support transportation, supply chains, and local infrastructure.

sources.title: Sens. Rev. Warnock, Ossoff Delivering Resources for Key Upgrades at Georgia Ports
News: Sen. Ossoff Delivering Funding Through Historic Chattahoochee River Act for Water Infrastructure Project Planning

candidates.donor_summary: Raised approximately $171.9M through his campaign committee and leadership PAC combined in the 2019–2024 cycle, with the largest disclosed contributor being Google Inc. ($1.10M). His donor base is dominated by Democratic/liberal giving, along with large totals from retired donors, education, lawyers/law firms, and health professionals; major corporate contributors also include Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta. Confidence: high.
candidates.voting_summary: Incumbent U.S. senator; GovTrack identifies him as a Democrat serving since Jan. 20, 2021. Public roll-call and scorecard sources show a consistently Democratic voting pattern, with recent Senate votes and GovTrack report cards documenting his participation in major 2025–2026 legislative actions. Confidence: high.

candidates.name: Buddy Carter
candidates.summary: Buddy Carter is a Republican U.S. representative from Georgia's 1st Congressional District and a former Georgia state legislator. He launched a campaign for the U.S. Senate in 2025, choosing to run for the open seat held by Jon Ossoff rather than seek reelection to the House.

Healthcare.stance: Carter says his health-care focus is on lowering costs, increasing price transparency, and improving access, especially for maternal and rural care. He has also backed efforts to address pharmacy benefit manager abuses and has introduced maternal-health legislation.

sources.title: Health Care : U.S. Representative Buddy Carter
PBM Abuses : U.S. Representative Buddy Carter
Carter bill preventing maternal deaths and improving health outcomes for mothers heads to Senate

Economy.stance: Continuing to advocate for pro-growth, pro-business economic policies, including deregulation and policies favorable to small businesses; explicitly endorses and credits President Trump's economic record as a model, arguing Trump's policies delivered Georgians jobs and growth.

sources.title: Trump's Home-Run Economic Policies Delivered for Georgians in ...
Georgia GOP U.S. Rep. Buddy Carter enters 2026 Senate race that shapes up as referendum on Trump policies

Climate/Energy.stance: Carter supports expanding domestic energy production, lowering energy prices, and reducing dependence on foreign energy sources. His official issue page emphasizes an all-of-the-above approach, and his office has highlighted legislation to improve energy efficiency and reverse Biden-era air-quality rules.

sources.title: Energy : U.S. Representative Buddy Carter
Environment : U.S. Representative Buddy Carter
Carter leads bill reversing Biden's jobs-killing air quality standards

Reproductive Rights.stance: Carter supports abortion restrictions and describes his position through a pro-life framework. His office has highlighted votes and actions to strengthen protections for the unborn and has opposed the Women's Health Protection Act.

sources.title: Carter Votes to Strengthen Protections for the Unborn
Vote Record : U.S. Representative Buddy Carter

Immigration.stance: Carter supports stricter immigration enforcement, including more cooperation with ICE and opposition to sanctuary-city policies. He has urged expanding ICE presence in Georgia and introduced legislation to require local cooperation with federal immigration authorities.

sources.title: Carter to force "sanctuary" cities to comply with ICE in new bill
As criticism of ICE mounts, Carter urges expansion to Georgia

Guns & Safety.stance: Carter presents himself as a strong supporter of Second Amendment rights and opposes gun-control measures he says would burden lawful gun owners. His office has framed gun policy around protecting legal gun use and arming servicemembers.

sources.title: Second Amendment : U.S. Representative Buddy Carter
Carter Acts to Protect Servicemembers

Foreign Policy.stance: Carter supports military action against Iran when he says Americans are threatened, while opposing war-powers limits on the president. He has said he does not support sending U.S. ground troops into Iran.

sources.title: Carter statement on military combat operations in Iran
Rep. Buddy Carter says he does not support ground troops in Iran

Social Justice.stance: Carter's public messaging emphasizes conservative themes such as border security, parental rights, and alignment with Donald Trump rather than a distinct social-justice agenda. His campaign and office materials do not present a separate social-justice platform.

sources.title: Buddy Carter For Senate
Georgia GOP U.S. Rep. Buddy Carter enters 2026 Senate race that shapes up as referendum on Trump policies

Education.stance: Carter says education decisions should be made by families, teachers, communities, and states rather than the federal government. He has emphasized parental rights and school choice in his official education materials and campaign messaging.

sources.title: Education : U.S. Representative Buddy Carter
School choice email from Representative Buddy Carter

Tech & AI.stance: Carter's tech policy emphasis is on child online safety. He has backed bipartisan legislation to require large social media platforms to monitor dangerous online activity involving minors and to let parents use third-party safety software.

sources.title: Carter celebrates Committee passage of his bill to protect children’s online safety
E&C holds hearing on Carter's bill protecting kids' online safety

Election Reform.stance: Carter supports stricter election rules, including documentary proof of citizenship and photo ID to vote. He has praised the SAVE America Act and backed measures to verify voter rolls and tighten absentee-ballot requirements.

sources.title: Carter lauds House passage of SAVE America Act
H.R.7296 - SAVE America Act

Local Issues.stance: Carter has focused locally on public safety and immigration enforcement, especially increasing ICE cooperation and presence in Georgia. He has also tied local concerns to crime, border enforcement, and sanctuary-city policies.

sources.title: Carter to force 'sanctuary' cities to comply with ICE in new bill
US Rep. Buddy Carter urges ICE expansion to Georgia
Georgia lawmaker calls for ICE surge after Savannah death

candidates.donor_summary: Raised approximately $2.58M through his campaign committee and leadership PAC in the 2023–2024 cycle. His donor profile is led by health professionals and pharmaceuticals/health products, with notable support from AIPAC ($35,666), Daniel Defense ($16,000), and the National Community Pharmacists Association ($14,000); PAC money made up the majority of funds. Confidence: high.
candidates.voting_summary: Incumbent U.S. representative; GovTrack identifies him as a Republican serving Georgia’s 1st District since 2015. His House voting record and scorecards show strong alignment with the Republican caucus, including votes on appropriations, border/security, and other party-line measures in 2025–2026. Confidence: high.

Healthcare.stance: Collins has opposed extending the Affordable Care Act's enhanced premium tax credits, voting against an extension in January 2026. His record on healthcare in Congress has generally aligned with Republican efforts to limit ACA subsidy expansions.

sources.title: U.S. House votes to extend Affordable Care Act health care subsidies
Democrats criticize Georgia US Senate challengers for end of health insurance subsidies

Economy.stance: Collins' campaign emphasizes a pro-Trump, pro-growth message centered on jobs, working-class voters, and support for businesses. His official campaign materials present him as a candidate who has worked for a living and created jobs.

sources.title: Mike Collins for Senate – Join the ride!
Georgia Congressman Mike Collins enters U.S. Senate race with pro-Trump message

Climate/Energy.stance: Collins supports rolling back federal emissions mandates, including the Diesel Truck Liberation Act, which would bar EPA emissions rules affecting diesel trucks and some farm equipment. He has argued such regulations raise costs for rural workers and businesses.

sources.title: Representative Mike Collins Introduces the Diesel Truck Liberation Act
S.3007 - Diesel Truck Liberation Act of 2025

Reproductive Rights.stance: Collins has a record of opposing abortion rights and supporting measures that restrict abortion access. Reproductive Freedom for All's scorecard cites his votes on abortion-related amendments and legislation, including the Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act.

sources.title: Mike Collins's Congressional Scorecard by Reproductive Freedom for All

Immigration.stance: Collins supports hard-line immigration enforcement and has publicly backed ICE-related detention efforts in Georgia. In 2026 he commented on the Social Circle ICE detention site and said he supports ICE while urging DHS to address local concerns.

sources.title: U.S. Rep. Mike Collins comments on DHS purchase of Social Circle warehouse for ICE detention center
Warehouse in process of being sold to DHS, Collins tells Social Circle
Rep. Collins issues statement regarding proposed ICE detention site in Social Circle

Guns & Safety.stance: Collins is aligned with gun-rights positions and has not been identified as supporting new gun restrictions in the reviewed campaign material. No separate 2026 Senate gun-policy platform was found.

sources.title: Mike Collins for Senate – Join the ride!
Georgia Republican Mike Collins seeks to challenge Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff in 2026

Foreign Policy.stance: No public position found on a detailed 2026 Senate foreign-policy platform. His available congressional record includes votes on war-powers resolutions to remove U.S. forces from Syria and Somalia, but he has not separately articulated a Senate campaign foreign-policy agenda in the reviewed materials.

sources.title: Rep. Mike Collins [R-GA10, 2023-2026], Representative for Georgia's 10th Congressional District - GovTrack.us

Social Justice.stance: No public position found on a distinct social-justice platform in the reviewed campaign material. His campaign messaging is centered on conservative themes, Trump alignment, and working-class economic appeals.

sources.title: Mike Collins for Senate – Join the ride!
Georgia Congressman Mike Collins enters U.S. Senate race with pro-Trump message

Education.stance: Collins' public record emphasizes parental rights and conservative oversight of schools. His House issues page includes education as a priority area, and his campaign materials do not show a separate, detailed Senate education platform in the reviewed sources.

sources.title: Issues | Representative Collins
Mike Collins for Senate – Join the ride!

Tech & AI.stance: No public position found on a comprehensive AI or technology-regulation platform. The reviewed material shows campaign use of AI-generated content, but not a formal governance agenda.

sources.title: Georgia Rep. Mike Collins' campaign uses AI-generated deepfake of Sen. Jon Ossoff in tight Senate showdown
Ad featuring AI generated senator in Georgia highlights use of technology in political campaigns

Election Reform.stance: Collins supports stricter election rules and voter-verification measures, including the SAVE Act and voter ID messaging. His campaign has framed these measures as necessary to prevent noncitizen voting and strengthen election integrity.

sources.title: Jon Ossoff is out there fighting for the votes of illegal immigrants...
Georgians overwhelmingly support voter ID...
H.R.22 - SAVE Act

Local Issues.stance: Collins has backed legislation to roll back federal diesel-truck emissions mandates, arguing EPA rules increase costs and burden rural workers and businesses. This has been one of his clearest Georgia-specific policy messages in the Senate race.

sources.title: Representative Mike Collins Introduces the Diesel Truck Liberation Act
S.3007 - Diesel Truck Liberation Act of 2025

candidates.donor_summary: Raised approximately $1.70M through his campaign committee and leadership PAC in the 2023–2024 cycle. His donor profile is led by retired donors, with additional support from building materials/equipment, real estate, food & beverage, and leadership PACs; the top disclosed contributor was Georgia Crown Distributing ($26,400). Confidence: high.
candidates.voting_summary: Incumbent U.S. representative; GovTrack identifies him as a Republican serving Georgia’s 10th District since 2023. His voting record and scorecards indicate near-total alignment with the Republican caucus, including votes on border/security, appropriations, and other high-profile House roll calls in 2025–2026. Confidence: high.

voting_record.bill_description: Dooley is a 2026 Senate candidate without prior congressional or state legislative service. AP reporting also noted that he had not voted for years before running.

source.title: Derek Dooley didn't vote for years. Now he wants to be a Georgia senator | AP News

Healthcare.stance: Dooley says healthcare decisions should be driven by patients and doctors rather than insurance companies, bureaucrats, or Washington politicians. His campaign emphasizes transparency, patient choice, and access to quality care.

sources.title: Priorities - Dooley for Georgia

Economy.stance: Dooley frames his economic message around lower Washington interference, more jobs, higher wages, and conditions that let small businesses and innovators succeed. His campaign also says it wants to put Georgia first and support policies that strengthen the state and the country.

sources.title: Priorities - Dooley for Georgia
The Georgia First Contract

The Georgia First Contract

The Georgia First Contract

Immigration.stance: Dooley says a secure border and enforcing the law are part of making communities safe. His campaign ties immigration to border security and public safety rather than offering a detailed immigration policy plan.

sources.title: Priorities - Dooley for Georgia

Guns & Safety.stance: Dooley has not published a detailed gun-policy platform in the campaign materials reviewed. His public messaging on safety focuses more broadly on secure borders, law enforcement, and safe communities.

sources.title: Priorities - Dooley for Georgia

Foreign Policy.stance: Dooley says America should lead with strength and resolve, but the campaign materials reviewed do not lay out detailed positions on specific foreign-policy questions such as Ukraine, Iran, or military engagement.

sources.title: Priorities - Dooley for Georgia
Derek Dooley for U.S. Senate

Education.stance: Dooley says Georgians want a quality education and the skills needed for good-paying jobs, and his campaign links education to opportunity and workforce preparation. The reviewed materials do not include a detailed K-12 or higher-education policy plan.

sources.title: Priorities - Dooley for Georgia
Derek Dooley for U.S. Senate

Election Reform.stance: Dooley's Georgia First Contract calls for term limits, banning members of Congress from trading individual stocks or digital assets, and holding lawmakers accountable for keeping the government open and doing the job they were elected to do. The reviewed materials do not include a broader election-administration platform.

sources.title: The Georgia First Contract

Local Issues.stance: Dooley's campaign says he is running to put Georgia first and focus on issues that matter to Georgians, including safe communities, quality education, good-paying jobs, and the ability to afford a home and raise a family. The message is broad and state-centered rather than a detailed local policy agenda.

sources.title: Priorities - Dooley for Georgia
The Georgia First Contract

candidates.donor_summary: No campaign finance data found in public disclosures as of 2026-04-07. OpenSecrets/FEC search results identified his campaign and FEC filing page, but the available public pages did not provide a usable fundraising total or donor breakdown in the sources reviewed. Confidence: low.
candidates.voting_summary: Has not held legislative office, so there is no legislative voting record to report. His campaign website says he is a former football coach and lawyer, and his public policy evidence is limited to campaign statements and the 'Georgia First Contract,' which frames him as an outsider aligned with President Trump’s agenda. Confidence: medium.

candidates.name: John F. Coyne III
candidates.summary: John F. Coyne III is a Republican candidate in Georgia's 2026 U.S. Senate race. Public campaign material and local reporting identify him as a businessman and longtime Medina, Ohio, city council member who has also run for mayor there.

summary_sources.title: About John Coyne — Candidate for Mayor of Medina, Ohio
John Coyne for Mayor of Medina — Accomplishments and Goals

candidates.donor_summary: No campaign finance data found in public disclosures as of 2026-04-07. FollowTheMoney lists him as an individual entity, but the public page reviewed did not surface a usable Georgia Senate fundraising total or donor breakdown. Confidence: low.
candidates.voting_summary: Has not held legislative office, so there is no legislative voting record to report. Publicly available evidence instead points to local executive/legislative service in Medina, Ohio: his campaign site says he served 27 years on Medina City Council, including 13 years as council president, but the sources reviewed did not provide a detailed roll-call voting record. Confidence: medium.

flags.concern: The polling array contains six near-duplicate entries: entries 0-4 are identical Emerson College Polling matchups dated 2026-03-02 or 2026-03-05, differing only slightly in formatting and spacing. Entries 5-6 are distinct (entry 5 repeats the matchups with floats, entry 6 shows the Republican primary). This redundancy suggests a data-ingestion or deduplication error during profile assembly.
flags.suggestion: Remove duplicate entries 1-4, retaining only the first clean instance of the general-election matchup poll. Keep entries 5-6 if they represent distinct data points or collapse them into one canonical entry with appropriate metadata.

flags.concern: Derek Dooley's assistant coaching entry spans 1996–2006, and his Louisiana Tech head-coach role is 2007–2009, leaving no documented gap. However, the iteration notes state: 'Adjusted Derek Dooley's assistant-coach career entry to avoid overlap with his head-coaching years.' The current profile shows no overlap, so either the adjustment was successful and this note is outdated, or the note refers to an intermediate version. No factual error is evident in the final profile, but the note–profile mismatch may indicate an editing artifact.
flags.suggestion: Verify the final career dates are correct against the source (2010 UT Media Guide) and remove or clarify the iteration note if the adjustment was indeed completed.

flags.concern: The source URL for Buddy Carter's top donors (OpenSecrets contributors page) is specifically noted in the title as 'career House fundraising, not limited to 2026 Senate campaign.' The donor summary field correctly states 'in the 2023–2024 cycle,' which may predate or only partially overlap with his 2026 Senate campaign (launched May 2025). This is not an error — the iteration notes acknowledge the data gap — but users should be aware these figures reflect House fundraising history, not Senate-specific contributions.
flags.suggestion: Consider adding a brief parenthetical to the donor_summary or a note in the source title clarifying that these are career House figures, not 2026 Senate campaign–specific totals, if FEC 2026 Senate filings have been filed since the March 2026 snapshot date.

flags.concern: Mike Collins' top_donors array is empty with a note in iteration_notes explaining: 'Removed Mike Collins top_donors entries...because reviewed accessible sources did not provide directly retrievable, source-verifiable named top contributors for his Senate committee.' This is appropriate transparency, but the empty array combined with the donor_source_url (FEC link) may confuse readers into thinking the search was incomplete. The iteration note clarifies the reasoning.
flags.suggestion: No revision needed; the empty array with clear iteration-note explanation is the correct handling of a data-retrieval gap. Optionally, add a brief note to donor_summary ('No itemized Senate committee contributors available in reviewed FEC documents') for explicit user-facing clarity.

flags.concern: John F. Coyne III is included with minimal public information: no website, no career_history or education entries, no issue positions beyond stub entries marked 'No public position found,' and no voter registration record. The summary notes he is 'a longtime Medina, Ohio, city council member' but the profile provides no legislative voting record from Medina. It is not clear from the available sources whether Coyne is a serious, visible candidate in the Georgia race or a minor/fringe entrant.
flags.suggestion: Consider adding a note in the summary or elsewhere clarifying Coyne's visibility/viability status in the 2026 race, or note that he has not published a detailed policy platform. Alternatively, if he is a minor candidate with negligible traction, consider whether inclusion is warranted or if a footnote about 'other candidates' would be more appropriate.

flags.concern: Mike Collins' Foreign Policy stance is marked as low-confidence ('No public position found on a detailed 2026 Senate foreign-policy platform'). His House voting record includes war-powers votes (Syria, Somalia) from 2023, but the iteration notes correctly reframe these as '118th Congress' votes that 'may not fully reflect his 2026 Senate campaign positions.' This is appropriate caution, but foreign policy is an important Senate issue area. The low confidence is justified by the absence of campaign messaging.
flags.suggestion: No revision needed. The low-confidence designation with explicit documentation of the gap is the correct approach. Consider noting in the summary that Collins has not yet published a detailed foreign-policy platform.

flags.concern: Mike Collins' Tech & AI stance is marked low-confidence and notes only that 'no public position found on a comprehensive AI or technology-regulation platform' and that his campaign has 'used AI-generated content' (a deepfake ad). This is factually accurate but leaves Tech & AI as a gap. The news sources cited document campaign strategy, not policy.
flags.suggestion: No revision needed. The low-confidence flag is appropriate and the deepfake-ad context is relevant to campaign behavior. The absence of a formal AI-policy platform is correctly reported.

reviews.summary: This is a comprehensive and well-sourced candidate profile for Georgia's 2026 Senate race. The incumbent (Jon Ossoff) and three major Republican contenders (Buddy Carter, Mike Collins, Derek Dooley) are thoroughly documented with high-confidence issue stances backed by official campaign materials, Senate/House voting records, and credible news sources. The profile demonstrates strong epistemological discipline: the iteration notes show explicit verification against FEC.gov, Congress.gov, Senate.gov, and official biographical records. Minor gaps exist for lower-profile candidates (John Coyne) and some Republican candidates' policy positions, but these are appropriately marked as low-confidence with documented "no public position found" entries rather than speculated stances. Sourcing is current (March 2026) and heavily weighted toward primary sources (official Senate/House sites, government records, campaign websites). Language is neutral and factual. The polling data shows a minor structural issue (duplicate entries), but the substantive information is clear.

flags.concern: There are multiple redundant entries (5-6) for the same Emerson College Polling/Nexstar Media survey from early March 2026. While the data is consistent, the duplication clutters the polling section.
flags.suggestion: De-duplicate polling entries to show only unique survey results or distinct demographic breakouts.

flags.concern: Candidate John F. Coyne III is listed as a Georgia Senate candidate, but all career history and official sources provided (coyneformayor.com, medinaoh.org) describe him as a local official in Medina, Ohio. While a Georgia-based news source (13WMAZ) is cited as including him in an election guide, there is a risk of a 'carpetbagger' scenario or a potential name-match confusion with a different local John Coyne.
flags.suggestion: Human reviewer should verify if John F. Coyne III has established residency in Georgia or if the 13WMAZ guide refers to a different individual with a similar name.

flags.concern: The iteration notes mention Derek Dooley's committee as C00914531, but the FEC link provided in the links array points to S6GA00408. My training data cannot reconcile these future-dated IDs.
flags.suggestion: Confirm which FEC ID is the active principal campaign committee for the 2026 cycle.

reviews.summary: This is an exceptionally high-quality research profile. The agent demonstrated rigorous self-correction in the iteration notes, specifically fixing education history and removing misattributed donor data. Sourcing is top-tier, utilizing official roll-call votes from Senate.gov and House.gov, specific FEC candidate IDs, and high-quality local reporting from the Atlanta Journal-Constitution and Georgia Recorder. The profile maintains a neutral tone throughout, accurately characterizing candidate platforms without partisan coloring.

flags.concern: The array contains multiple identical or near-identical entries for the same poll from Emerson College Polling/Nexstar Media, which may indicate redundancy in data aggregation rather than new information.
flags.suggestion: Consolidate duplicate entries to improve readability and avoid repetition without altering the underlying data.

reviews.summary: The profile is well-constructed with thorough sourcing from credible government, official, and news sites, ensuring internal consistency in candidate backgrounds, voting records, and issue stances. It handles gaps effectively by noting absences of public positions for less prominent candidates and maintains a neutral, nonpartisan tone throughout, making it a reliable and comprehensive resource for the 2026 Georgia Senate race.

polling_note: Recent public polling in the last 90 days has shown Jon Ossoff leading each of the main Republican contenders in hypothetical general-election matchups; the most recent publicly available survey located was Emerson College Polling/Nexstar Media, fielded Feb. 28-Mar. 2, 2026.

validation_grade.summary: Validated by 3/3 reviewers with an average score of 90/100.
